title: rubia edgeworthii
intro: Rubia edgeworthii, also known as the Himalayan madder or Indian madder, is a perennial plant native to the Himalayan region and parts of India. This plant is rich in anthraquinone glycosides, particularly rubiadin and rubadin-1, which are responsible for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Rubia edgeworthii include treating digestive issues, skin conditions, and wounds, as well as exhibiting antioxidant and antiviral properties. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions for medicinal use, and its root is also used in traditional Ayurvedic and Unani medicine to treat various ailments.
